title: 5 Filters to Know It’s God’s Voice
hook: Is it God—or just your thoughts? Run it through these 5 Bible-backed filters before you act.
script: Is it God—or just your thoughts? Run it through these 5 Bible-backed filters before you act. First, does it agree with Scripture? God’s voice never contradicts his Word. Hebrews 4:12 says the Word discerns thoughts. Second, does it aim at Christlike fruit—love, joy, peace, patience? Not fear, pride, or hurry. Third, do wise believers affirm it? Invite counsel. Fourth, notice your soul: steady peace or strangling pressure? Philippians 4:7 guards hearts. Fifth, providence: are doors opening without manipulation? God guides steps and stops. If one filter flags red, pause. Pray: Jesus, Shepherd, tune my ears, quiet my fears, align my feet with your way. Save this and test every nudge.

title: Stop Overthinking God’s Voice
script: You may be overthinking this. Anxiety shouts, the Spirit shepherds. In 60 seconds, test that decision. Breathe slow. Name it. Now ask: Does it clearly align with Scripture you can point to? Would acting on it grow Christlike fruit in you and others? Have your trusted mentors or community confirmed it without pressure? Do you sense settled, guarding peace rather than panic, like Philippians 4:7? Are circumstances aligning without you forcing outcomes? Overthinking uses distortions—catastrophizing, all-or-nothing, urgency. God’s voice is steady, pure, peaceable. John 10:27: My sheep hear my voice. If fear drives, wait and pray. If peace abides and counsel agrees, take the next small step.

title: When God Feels Silent: 5-Filter Guide
hook: If God feels silent right now, watch this.
script: If God feels silent right now, watch this. You asked for a sign, but the noise got louder. Here’s a calm way to listen. Open Scripture first; God will never lead you to disobey what he’s already said. Hebrews 4:12 cuts through motives. Then check fruit: Does this path look like Jesus—humble, loving, patient? Share it with a mature believer and invite honest counsel. Pay attention to your inner weather: peace that guards, or pressure that rushes. Philippians 4:7 marks God’s lane. Finally, providence: are doors opening without you forcing them? If not, it’s okay to wait. Pray: Lord, I surrender my timeline. Speak, and I’ll obey the next clear step.
